texto: The thing about me is – essentially, I’m a coward.I am. I can’t stand weird stuff, anything that’s not normal. […]I’m a coward, and I’ve faced it, and I’ve learn to accept it. And I’m okay with that, as long as nothing happens so I have to start feeling ashamed about it, or guilty.I don’t think my parents know this about me. […]But the other thing is, I just hate it when people are disappointed in me.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kristen D. Randle sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The thing about me is – essentially, I’m a coward.I am. I can’t stand weird stuff, anything that’…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, acceptance-of-oneself, cowardice, útil em momentos de cowardice e disappointment.

frase_original: The thing about me is – essentially, I’m a coward.I am. I can’t stand weird stuff, anything that’s not normal. […]I’m a coward, and I’ve faced it, and I’ve learn to accept it. And I’m okay with that, as long as nothing happens so I have to start feeling ashamed about it, or guilty.I don’t think my parents know this about me. […]But the other thing is, I just hate it when people are disappointed in me.
